programmes.show_description: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Charlotte Church, Melvin Odoom and Katherine Ryan all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Coronation Street stars Lucy Fallon, Julia Goulding, Jack P Shepherd and former Weatherfield regular Colson Smith go head-to-head in a battle to win big money for charity

programmes.show_name: STV Racing Live: Irish Grand National
programmes.show_description: Rishi Persad presents coverage of one of the biggest races in the Irish calendar from Fairyhouse, plus racing from Plumpton and Kempton Park. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Megan Nicholls, reports by Sally Ann Grassick and commentary from Mark Johnson

Ben Shephard hosts as Janette Manrara, Ade Adepitan and Sarah Millican answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

Bradley Walsh presents as Christine Ohuruogu, Max George, Maisie Smith and Owain Wyn-Evans pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
Rhona has a big decision to make, while Cain confides in Moira and Kerry is furious
Ronnie learns of Carl's latest ploy to extort cash from Debbie, Ben reels from the paternity revelation, Jodie joins David on his day off, and Hope taunts Will in front of Sam

programmes.show_name: I'm a Celebrity South Africa: Sneak Peek
programmes.show_description: A preview of the second series of the programme in which memorable campmates from past series of I'm A Celebrity...Get Me Out Of Here! compete against each other in challenges that are bigger and tougher than before - and more unforgiving
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

Lee Mack hosts the first of five special editions of the quiz in which the prize pot that is not won on one show is carried over to the next day's edition, meaning there could be up to £500,000 to be won. As usual, 100 contestants begin every show, but those who make it through to the final question return the next day to play the quiz again

Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for the £1 million prize - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder. Unlike classic Millionaire, each question is against the clock and there is no room for indecision - only one player will have a chance to win the top prize remaining on the ladder, but will they be able to do so?

programmes.show_name: I'm a Celebrity South Africa: Sneak Peek
programmes.show_description: A preview of the second series of the programme in which memorable campmates from past series of I'm A Celebrity...Get Me Out Of Here! compete against each other in challenges that are bigger and tougher than before - and more unforgiving
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

It's scanning time on the farm and the family are about to find out whether their three male tups have earned their keep and got the ewes in lamb, with all four children are on hand to help. Liz and Kelvin turn to their socials to drum up interest in their first farmers market, Marnie makes a decision on what prime cuts she wants to sell, and Milo helps his dad load up the Jacob sheep that are heading for the butcher

Stephen Mulhern hosts as contestants Brian Conley, Sue Perkins and Colin Salmon to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice
David Dickinson and his dealers Henry Nicholls, Stewart Hofgartner, Tim Hogarth and Mark Stevens assess antiques in Burton
Stephen Mulhern hosts the exciting and iconic gameshow as one of his brave contestants dares to face the infamous Banker for a chance of winning a life-changing cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh presents as Richard from Bradford, Grace from Portsmouth, Gordon from West Lothian and Eleanor from Broadstairs pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
By the Scottish National Party
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

The duo meet two engineering brothers who have built a fleet of giant robots and road trip to Osaka where they explore the area in go-karts. In the final leg of their time in Japan, the pair take in the sights, stumble across a group of eccentric rapping grannies, go head-to-head in pillow fighting and attempt to master the skills required to become a ninja
Cain is involved in an accident, while Jacob is left humiliated and Kerry confides in Jai
Debbie makes her presence felt at the hotel, Sam accuses Will of intimidation, Ben refuses to give Maggie the time of day, and Tim summons the courage to face Trisha

Ant and Dec introduce further coverage as the campmates are split into two groups, which causes drama and two new famous faces join them. The first eating trial of the series sees the celebs battling it out to win dinner that evening, with the losers having to make do with rice and beans

Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

Lee Mack hosts a special edition of the quiz in which the prize pot that is not won on one show is carried over to the next day's edition, meaning a potential jackpot of £500,000. What's more, anyone who makes it through to the final question automatically comes back to compete again the following day
